name: Montilla y La Campiña – circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Montilla traverse an undulating terrain of gentle hills and mounds, characteristic of Cordoba's Campiña Sur. The landscape is extensively covered by vineyards and olive groves, creating varied paths for running. Areas like Cerro Don Juan and Piedra Luenga offer natural refuges with Mediterranean scrub and unique geological features. The region's natural environment provides a rich biodiversity, making it suitable for outdoor activities like running. Best jogging routes around Montilla The most popular jogging route is Montilla y La Campiña – circular, a 7.9 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive experience of the Montilla countryside. Another top favourite among local runners is Climb to Las Useas loop from Montilla, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, providing a challenging workout through the local hills. Local runners also love the Montilla Town Center – Montilla loop from Montilla, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through the town center, often completed in about 45 minutes. Jogging around Montilla is defined by vineyards, olive groves, and gentle hills, offering a blend of agricultural landscapes and natural Mediterranean forest. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy town loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Montilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Montilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Montilla?
A: Montilla offers a diverse network of over 35 running routes. These paths traverse an undulating terrain of gentle hills, vineyards, and olive groves, providing varied options for runners of all levels.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Montilla?
A: Yes, Montilla has options for beginners. For an easier experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla Town Center – Montilla loop from Montilla&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 4.3 miles (6.9 km) long and leads through the town center, offering a less challenging run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Montilla's jogging trails?
A: The jogging trails in Montilla feature an undulating terrain of gentle hills and mounds, characteristic of Cordoba's Campiña Sur. You'll primarily run through extensive vineyards and olive groves, with some areas offering Mediterranean forest and unique geological formations like those found near Piedra Luenga.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in Montilla?
A: For those seeking a longer run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Las Useas loop from Montilla&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 11.5 miles (18.5 km) path with significant elevation gain. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24524681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Montilla loop from Montilla&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 10.4 miles (16.8 km).

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes in the Montilla area?
A: Absolutely. Many of Montilla's running paths are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000351250/montilla-y-la-campina-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla y La Campiña – circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 7.9 miles (12.6 km) and offers a comprehensive experience of the countryside.

Q: What are some scenic views or natural features I might encounter while running in Montilla?
A: Montilla's landscape is rich with scenic beauty. You'll run through endless fields of grapevines and olive groves, with the backdrop of gentle rolling hills. Areas like Cerro Don Juan offer Mediterranean scrub and a variety of fauna, while Piedra Luenga features unique dolomitic rock formations. The Fountains Route provides a blend of nature, history, and traditional rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the running routes?
A: Yes, you can explore several interesting points near the running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5358839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla Town Center&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight. Further afield, you might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5358247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Espejo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of Fernán Núñez&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;olive and almond fields&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Montilla suitable for dog-friendly running?
A: Montilla's natural environment, with its agricultural paths and open countryside, generally offers good opportunities for running with dogs. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or protected natural spaces like Cerro Don Juan, to protect local wildlife and crops.

Q: What is the best season for running in Montilla?
A: The best seasons for running in Montilla are typically spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions to enjoy the vineyards, olive groves, and natural landscapes. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and sometimes wet.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Montilla?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Montilla, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the peaceful atmosphere of the vineyards and olive groves, and the opportunity to explore the region's natural beauty and historical elements.

Q: Are there any specific permits required to run on trails in Montilla?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for recreational running on the public agricultural paths and trails around Montilla. However, when running through natural areas like Cerro Don Juan or Piedra Luenga, it's important to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing the natural environment.
tips: Views of the typical landscape of the Cordoba countryside.

This monumental medieval castle originally had a trapezoidal shape with towers on both sides of its perimeter. The original structure was demolished and replaced in the 18th century by a large grain store with enormous ramps inside on the first two floors.

In the heart of the Cordoba countryside, this town is especially known for its vineyards, even having its own designation of origin: Montilla-Moriles. Olive trees and cereals are also cultivated. The town is located in a sea of hills among which the Sierra de Montilla timidly stands out.
